title: Ordering, Single Active Consumer, and Partitioning · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent Go integration team receives different topology, traffic, schema, tenant, dependency, and shutdown constraints and transfers the module into a new a keyed order and failover state machine in Go. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a keyed order and failover state machine in Go with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use single active consumer when one active delivery stream is required and account for failover, prefetch, recovery, and throughput limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Ordering Scope Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 3099 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits; retain screen-reader-readable topology and delivery evidence; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Ordering Scope Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Ordering Scope Contract, keep Ordering Scope Contract connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Single Active Consumer · changed case · Lab Single Active Consumer

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ordering Throughput Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
